Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-methionine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = S-methyl-L-methionine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:13761, ChEBI:CHEBI:57844, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:58252, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.12; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10449582};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the S-methylmethionine (SMM) biosynthesis from adenosyl-L-homocysteine (AdoMet) and methionine. SMM biosynthesis (by MMT1) and degradation (by HMT-1, HMT-2 and HMT-3) constitute the SMM cycle in plants, which is probably required to achieve short term control of AdoMet level. Also able to catalyze the selenium-methylmethionine (SeMM) from AdoMet and selenium-methionine (SeMet). May play a role in phoem sulfur transport; such function is however not essential. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:10449582, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12376649}.
